$comment: Era-appropriate transformative-treatment presets for the animated-history-map (maps) channel. The maps channel is NOT Midnight Magnates noir — each video's grade + frame derive from the SUBJECT'S era/culture (see memory video_specific_coloring). These presets REUSE the shared clip_treatment engine (tools/clip_treatment/): every `filter` is a real recipe in tools/clip_treatment/filters.py, every `audio` is a real recipe in tools/clip_treatment/audio_recipes.py, every `frame` is either one of the 8 approved frames or an era frame HTML shipped beside the engine in tools/clip_treatment/frames/. DO NOT duplicate the engine — call ClipTreatment(operation=apply_filter|apply_audio|wrap_in_frame).
selection_rule: Pick the era preset whose [year_min, year_max] window contains the asset's source year AND whose culture matches the subject. If no preset fits, propose a new one (new frame HTML beside the engine + a row here) rather than defaulting to MM noir.

lincoln_1865.label: American Civil War / 1865 parchment
lincoln_1865.rationale: 1865 has NO film and NO recorded audio, so any third-party media is a still engraving/photo (Currier & Ives, Gardner) or a modern reenactment clip. sepia_archive (desaturated, warm-shifted, grainy) reads period-true; the aged-parchment Civil-War frame (oval gilt mat, foxed paper, telegraph-bar caption slot) matches 1865 print culture. tv-vintage CRT scanlines on an 1865 engraving would read anachronistic / AI-slop (clip-treatments.md period rule). Use grade_cyan_orange/noir frames ONLY for modern-era maps videos, never for 1865.

gilded_age_1880_1905.label: Gilded Age / early-broadcast-adjacent
gilded_age_1880_1905.rationale: Garfield (1881) / McKinley (1901) sit in the newsprint-halftone era; coarse halftone on motion + sepia on stills + a period broadsheet frame. Still no synced film/audio, so treat as stills unless a verified reenactment clip is used.

broadcast_1945_1989.label: Mid-century broadcast (CRT-true)
broadcast_1945_1989.rationale: The ONLY maps-channel era where tv-vintage CRT scanlines are period-true. JFK/Zapruder (1963), Reagan attempt (1981) are genuine broadcast/film, so the MM locked video default (grade_cyan_orange + tv-vintage) applies here. Earlier eras must NOT borrow this look.
